title: Pittsburgh's Loudest Instagram Feeds
subtitle: Ten small-business accounts the city actually showed up for this quarter.
angle: The Pittsburgh small-business Instagram accounts whose audience is genuinely engaged this quarter, one recent post each. Ranked, not scored.
intro:
Spot of Coffee runs an account with a starter-pack follower count and a comment section that reads like a group chat. Over 800 people had something to say about one closed-on-Mondays giveaway post. The post sits at number two this issue. That's the kind of audience this list is about.

These are the ten Pittsburgh small-business feeds whose followers actually do something. They comment. They share. They watch the whole reel. Most of these accounts are not big. Delanie's in Shadyside takes the top spot on one honky-tonk photo that pulled the kind of likes giveaways usually need a $100 gift card for. Page's lands at three. The seventy-five-year-old ice cream stand in Arlington dropped an April Fool's pickle-split sundae that read like a citywide RSVP. Nan Xiang's South Side dumpling counter shows up twice off a small follower base.

National chains are out. So is anything we suspected got indexed by accident. What's left is a list of operators who know who they're posting for. Issue 02 will widen the pool and name the feeds that hold their spot.
